answer:
**Vyavahāra** (Sanskrit: व्यवहार), often translated as "legal procedure," "judicial process," or "civil dispute resolution," refers to the systematic adjudication of interpersonal conflicts, typically contrasting with *ācāra* (personal ethical conduct) and forming one of the core divisions of *dharmaśāstra* alongside *ācāra* and *prāyaścitta* (expiation). It encompasses rules for plaints, replies, evidence (documents, witnesses, ordeals), and decisions in disputes, often organized under 18 principal "titles" or "topics" (*vyavahāramūlaka* or *pañcadaśa* in some texts, but standardized as 18 across major Smṛtis).[^1][^2][^3]

### Definition and Structure
In the Smṛti corpus, vyavahāra is the "dispute arising from a demonstrable cause" (*hetusambandhavyavahāra*) when *dharma* is disrupted, serving to "remove various doubts" (*vi-* "various," *ava-* "doubt," *-hāra* "removal").[^4] It has "four feet": plaint (*pūrva-pakṣa*), reply (*uttara-pakṣa*), evidence (*pramāṇa*), and decision (*nirṇaya*). Procedures emphasize impartiality, with the king or appointed judge (*dharmādhikāri*) presiding alongside assessors (*sabhā-sad*), prioritizing *śruti/smṛti*, custom (*deśācāra*), and royal edict.[^5][^6]

The 18 titles/topics (*vyavahāra-mūlakas*), consistently listed across texts, are:
1. Non-payment of debt (*ṛṇa-pratidenā*)
2. Deposits (*nikṣepa*)
3. Partnerships (*saṁbhāya-samutthāna*)
4. Resumption of gifts (*datta-āpradāna*)
5. Non-performance of service (*abhūpeta-āyuśrūṣā*)
6. Non-payment of wages (*vetana-pratidenā*)
7. Sale without ownership (*asvāmi-vikrayā*)
8. Non-delivery of sold goods (*vikrīyā-asampadāna*)
9. Rescission of purchase/sale (*krīṭānuśaya*)
10. Breach of contract (*saṁvidvyatikrama*)
11. Boundary/land disputes (*sīmā-vivāda*)
12. Relations between man and woman (*strī-puṁsaṁgrahaṇa*)
13. Inheritance/partition (*dāyabhāga*)
14. Violent crimes (*sāhasa*)
15. Verbal assault (*vākpāruṣya*)
16. Physical assault (*daṇḍapāruṣya*)
17. Gambling (*dyūta*)
18. Miscellaneous (*prakīrṇa*)

These are elaborated with sub-rules on proof, ordeals (*divya*), fines (*daṇḍa*: lowest/middle/highest amercements), and punishments (fines, corporal, capital, adjusted by *varṇa*, intent, and value).[^7][^8][^9][^10]

### Key Principles Across Smṛtis
- **Hierarchy of Authority**: *Śruti/smṛti* > custom (*deśācāra*) > royal edict (*rājaśāsana*). Conflicts resolved by reason (*nyāya*).[^11][^12]
- **Proof (*pramāṇa*)**: Human (witnesses, documents, possession/inference); divine (balance, fire, water, poison, consecrated water ordeals – used when human proof fails or in violence/theft). Witnesses (3–7 learned Brahmins preferred); documents must specify details (date, parties, amount).[^13][^14][^15]
- **Procedure**: Plaint written first; reply (denial/confession/special plea/former judgment); evidence; decision. Changes mid-process fined. Settlements encouraged if equitable.[^16][^17]
- **Punishments**: Scaled by *varṇa* (Brahmin: fines/branding/banishment, no death; others harsher), offense gravity (e.g., theft: 5–11x value; violence: death for highest). King shares fines (1/6th–1/20th).[^18][^19]
- **Protections**: Minors/aged/sick/ascetics exempt from summons; women via guardians; no ordeal for weak/Brahmins in certain cases.[^20]

### Coverage in Major Smṛtis
The Falsafa corpus's Indic Smṛtis treat vyavahāra extensively, especially Ch. 8 of *Manusmṛti* (judicial proceedings/18 titles), Ch. 1 of *Nāradasmṛti* (detailed 18 topics/procedures), Ch. 2 of *Yājñavalkya Smṛti* (*vyavahārādhyāya*), Ch. 3+ of *Kātyāyana Smṛti* (80 chs. on procedures), and Ch. 1 of *Bṛhaspati Smṛti* (18 topics/courts). *Viṣṇu Smṛti* covers supporting elements (Chs. 6–14: debts/documents/witnesses/ordeals).[^21][^22]

Vyavahāra ensures social order (*lokakṣema*), with the king as upholder of *daṇḍa* (punishment). The corpus lacks full *vyavahāra-pada* for some (e.g., *Pārāśara*), but principles align.[^23]

[^1]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-962160).
[^2]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 8, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-87f976).
[^3]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para.](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-d2bcad).
[^4]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, Ch. 3, [para.](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/03/translation/#p-20153b).
[^5]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-302671).
[^6]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-ef0ca8).
[^7]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 8, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-87f976).
[^8]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-99f176).
[^9]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, Ch. 3, [para.](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/03/translation/#p-bb508e).
[^10]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-d81171).
[^11]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para.](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-048b9a).
[^12]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-6a3e92).
[^13]: Viṣṇu Smṛti, Chs. 7–8, [para.](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/07/translation/#p-08aad7).
[^14]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 8, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-34dde1).
[^15]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, Ch. 23–38 (proof/ordeals), [ch.](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/23/translation/).
[^16]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/#p-db9f30).
[^17]: Bṛhaspati Smṛti, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-971be3).
[^18]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 8, [para.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/#p-1db2a4).
[^19]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Ch. 2, [para.](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-286928).
[^20]: Kātyāyana Smṛti, Ch. 12, [para.](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/12/translation/).
[^21]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 8 (full ch. on vyavahāra), [ch.](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/08/translation/).
[^22]: Nāradasmṛti, Ch. 1 (primary vyavahāra text), [ch.](/works/unknown-naradasmrti-7650d2/01/translation/).
[^23]: Viṣṇu Smṛti lacks full vyavahāra but supports via Chs. 6–14; corpus has 37 works, vyavahāra prominent in 6+ Smṛtis listed.
